textPlain: Ironic (at least to me) that trying to go to the URL results in a message that I am blocked because it thinks I'm a bot. I assume for the same reason I get that from the NYT (because I tend to use a VPN... horrors).At least they are both consistent in that trying to sign up for a subscription leads to the same message.

I think the rest of the world have realized, even before most Americans, just how cheap our politicians can be bought for. Not billions, not millions, but for a few thousand dollars you can get a member of congress to scuttle a bill or praise a despot. A few million dollars gets you a national social media disinformation campaign. And for $200k, membership into Mar-a-Lago “country club”.Our government sold out our ideals for cheap.
I guess America finds old media less useful for its propaganda than online bots.
meta propaganda, from one of the best.

Yawn. The idea that we were somehow providing access to "truth" and not our version of it was always a bit of a farce. We meddle too much to not undercut potential trust.The atlantic itself is a megaphone for american interests. There's a revolving door between DC and these rags
As if your government ever had genuine ideals other than profit...
old media is controlled by one groupnew media by the otherthus both types are dumping on the other
